text: From ideas to reliable intelligent software
Models + data + APIs + product engineering + governance
Jump into any section, return home anytime, and keep the day moving.
Tip: use slideshow mode. Every top-level card, footer pill, and HOME button is clickable.
A balanced day: vision, foundations, demos, engineering depth, governance, and roadmap.
Use demos early. Explain architecture after the audience has seen the behavior.
Keep 30 to 45 minutes available for deeper Q&A, setup delays, or audience-led discussion.
Use HOME for navigation and speaker notes for talk tracks.
AI is not a feature bolted on at the end. It is a capability layer inside a complete product system.
"AI is a capability layer: data, models, APIs, UX, evaluation, security, and operations working together."
Retrieval, tools, jobs, data, APIs, state
Permissions, evals, audit logs, human approval
Six pillars show breadth without turning the workshop into a random collection of experiments.
A production-minded AI system coordinates UX, APIs, orchestration, models, retrieval, data, integrations, and operations.
Web app, dashboards, chat, admin console
Workflow engine, agent planner, queues, approvals
SQL, files, CRM, ERP, email, calendar
RBAC, audit logs, evals, monitoring, CI/CD
Retrieval augmented generation grounds answers in trusted sources instead of asking the model to guess.
Answer directly, cite sources, separate fact from interpretation, show uncertainty, and refuse unsupported questions.
A private knowledge assistant that answers from approved documents with citations and refusal behavior.
Chunking, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, reranking, citations, feedback loop, evaluation set.
Convert unstructured documents into validated, reviewable, API-ready structured data.
Extraction fields are defined up front and validated before export.
Low-confidence values are routed to human review instead of hidden.
Validated JSON payloads can move into workflow systems safely.
Show that applied AI is broader than chat: forecasting, anomaly detection, scoring, and decision support.
Clean data, features, split, train, test, serve, monitor.
Use metrics to show that ML is measurable, not magical.
Move from answers to controlled actions through typed tools, approvals, API execution, and audit logs.
Read tools and write tools are separate. External writes require approval.
Every tool has a schema, validation, timeout, retry behavior, and audit payload.
Show proposed actions before execution. Keep demos safe and inspectable.
AI becomes useful when it can safely read context, call tools, and write approved outcomes.
Permission first, then retrieval, then action.
Images, screenshots, charts, forms, and video frames become first-class inputs for intelligent workflows.
Use AI to understand code, generate tests, review diffs, explain logs, and speed up delivery.
Index a repo, explain modules, map dependencies.
Create focused unit tests and API docs.
Inspect diffs for bugs, regressions, missing tests.
Summarize logs and propose next checks.
Human review remains the gate: AI proposes, engineers verify, tests prove.
The workshop should prove full-stack competence, not only model familiarity.
Typed APIs, auth, validation, background jobs
SQL, object storage, vector search, cache
LLMs, embeddings, vision, speech, classical ML
The mature question is not only what the model can do. It is how we know it works.

Golden Q&A
Human review
Schema and source checks
Red-team prompts

Latency and cost
Refusal rate
Tool success
Feedback review
Trust comes from permission checks, audit logs, refusal behavior, privacy controls, and human approval.
Every connector gets only the scope it needs.
Filter content before the model sees it.
External writes require explicit human approval.
Store prompts, tool calls, decisions, and outcomes.
Retrieved text cannot override system/tool rules.
Rate limits, quotas, caching, and model routing.
Pick a portfolio that proves both model depth and software integration depth.
Best mix: two high-integration demos, two high-AI-depth demos, one live engineering proof point.
A bounded live build proves real skill without risking the entire workshop.
Paste text, chunk it, retrieve relevant sections, generate cited answer.
Define schema, validate input, dry-run action, request approval, log result.
Load CSV, profile columns, chart metrics, generate grounded summary.
Rule: tiny scope, rehearsed starter code, mock mode, and a recorded fallback.
Every impressive live demo needs a calm backup path.
dry-run mode and visible tool payload
labeled expected output and review queue
Short videos support live demos, compress complex flows, and protect the session from network/API surprises.
Keep total video time around 12 to 18 minutes. Use videos as backup and emphasis, not as the whole workshop.
From Wednesday, April 22, 2026 to workshop day on Tuesday, April 28, 2026.
Network, display, mock mode, local data, backup videos, tabs, commands.
Turn the workshop energy into pilots, integrations, governance, and measurable production value.
Select use cases, define success metrics, build bounded prototypes.
Pilot with users, connect APIs, create evaluation sets, review security.
Productionize winners, monitor quality/cost, create reusable AI platform patterns.
The advantage is not access to models. The advantage is knowing how to turn models into reliable capability.
Which workflows should be productionized first?
How do we connect AI safely to APIs?
How do we measure quality and cost?
What data and governance are required?
Models are powerful. Reliable AI capability comes from the system around them.
